Module 4:
Offer the Prize
www.getwsodownload.com

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Introduce Commercial Terms
It is now time to introduce commercial terms.
We have taken control of the social environment.
We raised our status.
We gave a compelling pitch.
We used intrigue to reboot the mind.
www.getwsodownload.com

You’re Now in the O Module
Buyers need to understand that they must be good customers.
No counterparty risk for you.
Cash is a commodity.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Three Phases In
It’s more than show and tell.
You are here to make a deal happen.
It’s time to propose something concrete and actionable.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Despite Everything, You’re There to Close Business
In order to do that, you need to offer your buyer terms.
Price, delivery schedule, order volume, fees, etc.
Elegantly unfold everything that the buyer gets when he pushes the buy button.
First, let’s see the new map for pitching.
www.getwsodownload.com

[New map for pitching.]

-- 7 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Welcome To the Next Phase
Here we introduce a new term: hot cognition.
1. What is a hot cognition?
2. How do you create a hot cognition?
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Hot Cognition
Remember: this approach turns pitches and business development/sales into a social activity.
A buyer can like a deal—and you—without knowing much about it.
What is a hot cognition?
Deciding you like something before you fully understand it.
That’s why we create pitches in this way.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Practical Hot Cognition
You already do this.
When buying a car.
We are not rational, analytical animals.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Fool Ourselves
We use our gut—but fool ourselves with analysis after the fact.
We change/skew our analysis in order to support what we want.
Most people don’t understand this
But you will work hard to create this feeling of wanting.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Wanting
Think about you and your friends.
You did not become friends after reasoned, rational analysis.
You did not choose which sports you enjoy in this way.
Sports you watch.
People you love.
???
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Moving Hot Cognition to Your Business Life
Our research serves to justify our choices.
We don’t need facts and information to convince ourselves. We know what we like.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
How to Create Hot Cognition
Now that you understand the fundamentals, how do you do it?
Reveal the terms of the deal in a four-frame hot cognition stack.
Implement frames.
Stacked one after another.
Create wanting.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Liking Versus Wanting
“Like” is not the goal.
Liking is cold, reasoned, measured.
Wanting is hot, and poised for movement.
Stack the following frames...
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Four-Frame Stack Ingredients
1. Intrigue Frame
Used to introduce price.
2. Prize Frame
Used to introduce terms
3. Time Frame
Used to introduce minimum order volume.
4. Moral Authority Frame
Ties it all together, and wraps it in admirable values.
Can be any relevant deal term.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Introducing Price (Intrigue Frame)
“ John, the price is more than you want to pay, of course,
how could it ever be different, and it’s less than we
want to charge.
The Blue Plastic Frogs are $1000 each, there’s no way
around that.
But it’s super interesting. You are going to meet—
because of the order size you have to place—you’re
going to meet our chief engineer.
This guy flies to Afghanistan twice a year to find the dye
that covers our product. Afghanistan is the only place in
the world with the right blue.
So anyway, with the 1st delivery I’m going to bring him
along. He’s going to help with the install... ”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Introducing Terms (Prize Frame)
“ John, the price is more than you want to pay, of course,
how could it ever be different, and it’s less than we
want to charge.
The Blue Plastic Frogs are $1000 each, there’s no way
around that.
But it’s super interesting. You are going to meet—
because of the order size you have to place—you’re
going to meet our chief engineer.
This guy flies to Afghanistan twice a year to find the dye
that covers our product. Afghanistan is the only place in
the world with the right blue.
So anyway, with the 1st delivery I’m going to bring him
along. He’s going to help with the install... ”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Introducing Time Constraint (Time Frame)
“ John, the price is more than you want to pay, of course,
how could it ever be different, and it’s less than we
want to charge.
The Blue Plastic Frogs are $1000 each, there’s no way
around that.
But it’s super interesting. You are going to meet—
because of the order size you have to place—you’re
going to meet our chief engineer.
This guy flies to Afghanistan twice a year to find the dye
that covers our product. Afghanistan is the only place in
the world with the right blue.
So anyway, with the 1st delivery I’m going to bring him
along. He’s going to help with the install... ”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 19 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Introducing Values (Moral Authority Frame)
“ John, the price is more than you want to pay, of course,
how could it ever be different, and it’s less than we
want to charge.
The Blue Plastic Frogs are $1000 each, there’s no way
around that.
But it’s super interesting. You are going to meet—
because of the order size you have to place—you’re
going to meet our chief engineer.
This guy flies to Afghanistan twice a year to find the dye
that covers our product. Afghanistan is the only place in
the world with the right blue.
So anyway, with the 1st delivery I’m going to bring him
along. He’s going to help with the install... ”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 20 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
What You’ve Done
Intrigue frame around price.
Prize frame around terms.
Time frame around decision deadline.
Moral authority frame around values to tie it all together.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 21 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
The Script: Pocket Version
“ John, the price is more than you want to pay, of course,
how could it ever be different, and it’s less than we
want to charge.
The Blue Plastic Frogs are $1000 each, there’s no way
around that.
But it’s super interesting. You are going to meet—
because of the order size you have to place—you’re
going to meet our chief engineer.
This guy flies to Afghanistan twice a year to find the dye
that covers our product. Afghanistan is the only place in
the world with the right blue.
So anyway, with the 1st delivery I’m going to bring him
along. He’s going to help with the install... ”
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 22 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Frame Stacking
You will develop and add your own.
The structure is the same:
[Frame] + [term/deal condition]
Hot Cognition
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 23 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Hot Versus Cold Cognition
Chocolate versus spinach.
Similarly, that’s the test of your pitch.
Do they want it? The way people want chocolate?
How much “thinking” does your audience need to a sign a preference value?
How much rational analysis you need before you decide: It’s good.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 24 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Introducing Risk
You don’t want to invite, and wait for, an evaluation of the price.
Your buyer will wander off into cold cognition.
Stacking frames creates instantaneous evaluation.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 25 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Feeling and Evaluating
Hot cognition is fast, and inside your brain, resides with the stem and midbrain.
Cold cognition is slow. It takes place in the analytical neocortex.
The neocortex is built to:
-take time
-collect data
-solve problems
Just the cold hard facts.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 26 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Triggering Hot Cognition
Most presentations today are set up to trigger cold cognition.
Use the four-frame stack.
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 27 of 30 --

PitchMastery.com
A PITCH ANYTHING PRODUCTION
Applications to Your Business
Take the following 5 steps:
1. Write your frames out on an index card. Include the commercial term.
2. Practice and rehearse.
Focus on a natural, conversational tone and pace.
3. Withdraw.
4. Leave the room.
Create a location shift. Tour the factory, grab a coffee, have lunch. Just move to a new environment.
and 5. ... 	The next phase...
Nail the Hookpoint
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 28 of 30 --

Module 4:
Offer the Prize
